metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `infra/scripts/observability.sh` and `infra/scripts/install-observability.sh` actually exist in the repo (deliverables of S2/S5); if they do not, add them explicitly to the S7 Files and AC list rather than treating the probe deployment as a pre-existing given.
findings.finding: The Round-12 plan-review remediation states the host health probe (`infra/scripts/observability.sh`, installed as `rebar-observability.timer` by `infra/scripts/install-observability.sh`) is 'already deployed in S2/S5', treating it as a verified prior-art deliverable S7 merely invokes. However, neither the `infra/` directory nor the named scripts appear to exist in the current repo snapshot — there is no `infra/` top-level directory — making this a capability assertion (pre-existing probe deployment) that cannot be verified from the codebase.
findings.location: Round-12 plan-review remediation > 'Probe deployment pinned' bullet; Files section > 'The host health-probe script (cron/systemd timer)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define where `BLOCKING_SEVERITIES` lives (module path, type, default value, and how it is loaded), or confirm it is a constant in the to-be-created `src/rebar/review_bot/config.py`, and add the minimal spec (e.g. `BLOCKING_SEVERITIES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({'critical', 'high'})`) to the plan so the implementer has an unambiguous authority for the threshold vocabulary.
findings.finding: The Round-12 advisory fix references `src/rebar/review_bot/config.py` with a `BLOCKING_SEVERITIES` config (default `{critical, high}`) as the threshold that drives the PASS/BLOCK decision in `code_review_decision`, but tool search confirms no `review_bot` directory or `config.py` exists anywhere under `src/rebar`. The enum vocabulary and its owning module are both absent from the codebase.
findings.location: Round-12 plan-review remediation > Advisory fixes folded in: 'blocking severity (default critical/high) in src/rebar/review_bot/config.py'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` (with `code_review_decision`) is a prerequisite delivered by S4b before S7 executes, and either add an explicit S4b dependency check to S7's sequencing or add the file to S7's Files section if S7 must create it.
findings.finding: The plan's Round-12 advisory fix and Round-11 remediation pin the code-review decision rule at `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision`, but tool verification confirms no `review_bot/` package or `adapter.py` exists in `src/rebar`. The plan treats this as an already-specified file (citing its module path, function name, and signature) but it is entirely absent from the codebase — the processing protocol's co-location claim is unverifiable.
findings.location: Round-12 advisory fixes: 'src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision'; Files section: not listed; Round-11 remediation (d251 epic): 'module src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the probe cadence: decide whether the `rebar-observability.timer` runs every 60 s or every 5 min, update the CloudWatch alarm period to match (e.g. `period = 300` with `evaluation_periods = 2` if 5 min), and state one authoritative interval throughout the plan.
findings.finding: The probe timer interval is stated as '60 s' in the Probe mechanism section (with the alarm configured for '2 consecutive 60 s periods') but as 'every 5 min' in the Round-12 remediation (describing the already-deployed `rebar-observability.timer`). These directly conflict: if the probe fires every 5 minutes, a 60-second alarm period will see missing data every cycle and misbehave.
findings.location: What > Probe mechanism section vs. Round-12 plan-review remediation > 'Probe deployment pinned'
findings.suggested_fix: If the probe is already deployed at 5-min cadence (S2/S5), set `period = 300, evaluation_periods = 2` in the CloudWatch alarm. Update the Probe mechanism section to read '...runs every 5 min...' and remove the '60 s' statement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Files section whether `monitoring.tf` CREATES the DLM policy (if S1 did not) or REFERENCES S1's existing policy (e.g. via a `data` source or a Terraform test assertion), and align the AC wording to match — the current Files description and the Round-12 remediation directly contradict each other.
findings.finding: The Files section lists 'DLM snapshot lifecycle policy (retain_rule.count = 7)' as content to be created in `monitoring.tf`, but the Round-12 remediation states 'S1 OWNS the DLM policy... S7 only ASSERTS/monitors it (no second DLM resource).' An implementer cannot tell whether to CREATE a new `aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` resource in `monitoring.tf` or merely reference/data-source S1's existing one.
findings.location: Files section ('infra/terraform/monitoring.tf') vs. Round-12 plan-review remediation > 'Snapshot retention ownership'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the Files section to read: 'monitoring.tf — ... REFERENCES (via data source) the DLM lifecycle policy created in S1 and asserts retain_rule.count ≥ 1; no new aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy resource is created here.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the probe cadence with the alarm evaluation period: either (a) update the alarm to use a 5-minute period (2 consecutive periods = 10-minute detection window) to match the existing `rebar-observability.timer` at 5 min, or (b) explicitly state that S7 changes the timer to 60s and update `infra/scripts/install-observability.sh` accordingly — and confirm that the Round-12 'every 5 min' note refers to a different metric/timer than the GerritReachable probe.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the CloudWatch alarm as firing on 'GerritReachable < 1 for 2 consecutive 60 s periods' (implying a 60-second probe cadence), but the Round-12 advisory states the already-deployed probe is `rebar-observability.timer` running 'every 5 min'. These are contradictory: if the probe publishes every 5 minutes, the alarm will see only one metric point per 300 seconds, making '2 consecutive 60s periods' semantically different from intended (missing data points would trigger `treat_missing_data = breaching` prematurely, causing false-positive alarm triggers every period the probe hasn't yet published). The effective detection window and alarm sensitivity are fundamentally different depending on which cadence governs. An implementer inheriting the existing 5-min timer and setting up a 60s-period alarm will get spurious breaching alarms.
findings.location: ## What — 'Probe mechanism' sub-bullet: 'runs every 60 s ... CloudWatch alarm fires when GerritReachable < 1 for 2 consecutive 60 s periods'; Round-12 advisory: 'installed as the rebar-observability.timer systemd timer by infra/scripts/install-observability.sh (every 5 min)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the `monitoring.tf` Files section to remove the 'DLM snapshot lifecycle policy' entry and replace it with a note that S7 only *references* or verifies S1's DLM policy (e.g., via a `data.aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` data source or a runbook check) — consistent with the Round-12 advisory fix.
findings.finding: The Files section lists 'DLM snapshot lifecycle policy (`retain_rule.count = 7`)' as a deliverable to be created in `monitoring.tf`, but the Round-12 plan-review remediation explicitly states 'S1 OWNS the DLM policy [...] S7 only ASSERTS/monitors it (no second DLM resource).' This contradiction means an implementer following the Files section would create a duplicate `aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` in `monitoring.tf`, causing Terraform state conflicts, duplicate policies, and double-snapshot costs.
findings.location: Files section — `infra/terraform/monitoring.tf` bullet listing 'DLM snapshot lifecycle policy (`retain_rule.count = 7`)'; Round-12 plan-review remediation — 'Snapshot retention ownership' advisory fix
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files section, change the `monitoring.tf` bullet to omit the DLM resource and add: '(S1 owns the DLM lifecycle policy; `monitoring.tf` only reads its state via `data.aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` for the runbook verification step.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the probe cadence: choose one authoritative interval (60 s or 5 min), update `monitoring.tf` alarm parameters to match (`period = 300, evaluation_periods = 2` if 5 min), and state that single interval consistently throughout the plan body and the Round-12 remediation section.
findings.finding: The probe cadence stated in the plan body (60 s, with a CloudWatch alarm period of 60 s) directly contradicts the Round-12 remediation, which states the already-deployed `rebar-observability.timer` fires 'every 5 min'. If the probe fires every 5 minutes but `monitoring.tf` sets `period = 60` and `evaluation_periods = 2`, CloudWatch will see missing data for ~4 of every 5 minutes, causing the alarm to behave incorrectly — either triggering `treat_missing_data = breaching` falsely every cycle, or never reaching the 2-consecutive-data-point threshold.
findings.location: What > Monitoring > Probe mechanism section ('runs every 60 s... fires when GerritReachable < 1 for 2 consecutive 60 s periods'); Round-12 plan-review remediation > 'Probe deployment pinned' ('installed as the rebar-observability.timer systemd timer [...] (every 5 min)')
findings.suggested_fix: If the probe is already deployed at 5-min cadence (S2/S5, authoritative), update the Probe mechanism section to read 'runs every 5 min' and set `period = 300, evaluation_periods = 2` in the CloudWatch alarm in `monitoring.tf`. Remove the '60 s' cadence statement from the plan body.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan to state that `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` must be declared **in S1's resource block for the EBS data volume** (not added from `monitoring.tf`); change the `monitoring.tf` Files entry to say S7 *verifies* the attribute is present (e.g., via a `terraform state show` check in the runbook or a Sentinel/OPA policy), rather than 'adds/asserts' it.
findings.finding: `monitoring.tf` cannot add or assert `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` on the EBS data volume because that resource is declared in S1's file (e.g., `main.tf`). In Terraform HCL, a `lifecycle` meta-argument must appear inside the resource block where the resource is declared — no cross-file lifecycle injection exists. The plan's phrasing 'adds/asserts `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` on the stateful data-volume resource' in `monitoring.tf` is architecturally incorrect, meaning the blast-radius safeguard may silently not be in effect.
findings.location: Files section — `infra/terraform/monitoring.tf` bullet: 'adds/asserts `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` on the stateful data-volume resource'; AC line 2 (restore drill): 'The live data volume carries `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }`'
findings.suggested_fix: In S1's Terraform file, ensure the data volume resource contains `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }`. In the S7 plan, change the `monitoring.tf` Files bullet to: '`monitoring.tf` does NOT manage the data volume — it is declared in S1; the restore runbook includes a `terraform state show <volume_resource>` verification step to confirm `prevent_destroy = true` is in effect before any DR apply.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit CREATE step for the `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` SSM parameter: either a `aws_ssm_parameter` resource in `monitoring.tf` (with the value supplied as a sensitive Terraform variable or from a bootstrap script), or a documented pre-apply bootstrap command in the provision runbook that operators run before the first `terraform apply`.
findings.finding: The SSM parameter `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` is consumed by `monitoring.tf` via `data.aws_ssm_parameter` at apply time, but no step in the plan defines who creates this parameter, when, or with what value. If it does not exist when `terraform apply` runs, the apply fails with a data-source lookup error.
findings.location: Files section — `infra/terraform/monitoring.tf`; Round-7 remediation (SNS alert endpoint SSM path E2); AC line 3 (gate-down alert)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the provision runbook a pre-apply step: `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/alert-endpoint --value <email/webhook> --type SecureString --region us-east-1` (one-time, operator-supplied). Alternatively, manage it as an `aws_ssm_parameter` resource in `monitoring.tf` with `var.alert_endpoint` (marked `sensitive = true`) so the lifecycle is Terraform-owned and self-documenting.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the What > E2E proof bullet (and any other occurrences) to replace the stale `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` reference with `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision`, consistent with the Round-12 advisory correction.
findings.finding: The What section still references `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` as the source of the CODE-review verdict ('PASS'/'BLOCK'), but the Round-12 advisory fix explicitly corrects this to `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision`. The corrected location is never reflected back in the What body or the first E2E AC paragraph, so a developer reading top-to-bottom will encounter a stale — and now contradicted — file reference before reaching the correction.
findings.location: What > E2E proof paragraph (first bullet) and Round-12 plan-review remediation > advisory fixes
findings.suggested_fix: In the first E2E proof bullet, replace: "(see `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`)" with "(via `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision`, per Round-12 advisory fix)"

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the stale `src/rebar/review_kernel/decide.py` citation from the 'LLM-Review label-integer vocabulary' sub-bullet in ## What and replace it with the correct source: `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision` (the adapter over `review_code()`'s findings + `BLOCKING_SEVERITIES` severity threshold), consistent with the Round-12 remediation already stated later in the plan.
findings.finding: The primary 'What' section cites `src/rebar/review_kernel/decide.py` as the source of the CODE-review PASS/BLOCK verdict that populates the LLM-Review label, but this is a factually incorrect code citation. `decide.py` is the plan-review gate's Pass-3 deterministic decider (block_threshold arithmetic over binary sub-answers), not a code-review verdict adapter. The correct source — as the Round-12 remediation states — is `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision` over `review_code()`'s findings + severity threshold. The stale `decide.py` reference appears in the primary 'LLM-Review label-integer vocabulary' sub-bullet of 'What', which an implementer reads first; the Round-12 correction is buried in a later section. An implementer following only the 'What' spec would look in the wrong module for the code that gates LLM-Review votes.
findings.location: ## What — 'LLM-Review label-integer vocabulary' sub-bullet: '...CODE-review verdict (PASS/BLOCK, see src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section LLM-Review sub-bullet, change '(PASS/BLOCK, see src/rebar/review_kernel/decide.py)' to '(PASS/BLOCK via src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision — BLOCK if any finding is at/above BLOCKING_SEVERITIES threshold, else PASS)' to match the Round-12 remediation already stated in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the 'What > E2E proof > LLM-Review label-integer vocabulary' bullet to remove the stale `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` reference and replace it with `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision` (consistent with the Round-12 advisory fix already in the plan body), so the primary plan text and its own remediation section do not contradict each other.
findings.finding: The 'What > E2E proof' section cites `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` as the source of the CODE-review verdict (PASS/BLOCK), but codebase inspection confirms `decide.py` is the four-pass plan-review framework's Pass-3 deterministic kernel — it operates on validity×impact arithmetic and emits `block|advisory|dropped|indeterminate`, not a severity-threshold PASS/BLOCK for code review. The actual code-review verdict source is `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision` (established in the Round-12 advisory fix). The plan body asserts a capability (`decide.py` as the code-review verdict path) that the codebase refutes.
findings.location: What > E2E proof > 'LLM-Review label-integer vocabulary' bullet: '(PASS/BLOCK, see `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(PASS/BLOCK, see `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`)' with '(PASS/BLOCK via `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision` — severity-threshold rule over `review_code()` findings, per Round-12 advisory fix; `decide.py` is the plan-review Pass-3 kernel and is unrelated)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the stale `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` cross-reference from the 'What > E2E proof' section and replace it with the authoritative `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision` citation already established in the Round-12 advisory fix, so there is exactly one, consistent authority statement for the CODE-review verdict source.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What > E2E proof' section still references `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` as the source of the CODE-review verdict (PASS/BLOCK), but tool verification confirms decide.py is the four-pass plan-review Pass-3 deterministic kernel (its docstring: 'Pass-3 of the four-pass review framework: the DETERMINISTIC decision'), not the code-review verdict path. The Round-12 advisory fix corrects this to `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision`, but the 'What' section body was never updated and retains the stale, contradictory reference. An implementer following the 'What' section top-down will be directed to the wrong module.
findings.location: What > E2E proof section (plan body): '...the review-bot casts it from the CODE-review verdict (PASS/BLOCK, see src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py)...'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What > E2E proof' section, change '...CODE-review verdict (PASS/BLOCK, see src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py)...' to '...CODE-review verdict (PASS/BLOCK) from `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py::code_review_decision`...'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or a CLAUDE.md architecture section) capturing the rationale for: (a) the custom-metric cron/systemd probe vs. ALB-based health check (no-ALB topology), (b) the dual-alarm pattern (probe alarm + EC2 StatusCheckFailed backstop) and why both are needed, and (c) the two-tier snapshot strategy (crash-consistent DLM routine vs. on-demand quiesced for planned events) — so the design intent is discoverable independently of the story text.
findings.finding: The novel dual-alarm monitoring architecture (custom-metric probe alarm + EC2 StatusCheckFailed backstop) and the two-tier snapshot strategy (crash-consistent DLM vs. on-demand quiesced) are new architectural decisions documented only in operational runbooks, not in an ADR or CLAUDE.md update.
findings.suggested_fix: Create `infra/runbooks/adr-0011-monitoring-architecture.md` covering the three decisions above; cross-reference it from `monitoring.tf` as a header comment and from the upgrade runbook's 'probe health-endpoint contract' section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Files section to clarify that `infra/scripts/observability.sh` (deployed in S2/S5) is the probe script — S7's deliverable is to VERIFY it publishes to `Rebar/Gate/GerritReachable` and UPDATE it only if the namespace/metric-name differ, not to create a new file.
findings.finding: The Files section lists the host health-probe script as a new deliverable ('The host health-probe script... co-located with the host provisioning assets'), but the Round-12 remediation states it is 'already deployed in S2/S5' as `infra/scripts/observability.sh`. An implementer cannot determine whether S7 must create/modify the script or only verify it and integrate with `monitoring.tf`.
findings.location: Files section (probe script bullet) vs. Round-12 remediation > 'Probe deployment pinned'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the vague Files bullet with: 'Verify/update `infra/scripts/observability.sh` (deployed in S2/S5) to confirm it publishes `--namespace Rebar/Gate --metric-name GerritReachable`; no new probe script file unless the existing one requires correction.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Files section to reflect that the probe script (`infra/scripts/observability.sh` / `rebar-observability.timer`) is an EXISTING artifact from S2/S5 that S7 relies on (not a new deliverable), and clarify that S7's deliverable is the `monitoring.tf` alarm/metric wiring against that existing probe.
findings.finding: The Files section lists 'The host health-probe script (cron/systemd timer)' as a deliverable of S7, but the Round-12 plan-review remediation states it is already deployed in S2/S5 as `infra/scripts/observability.sh` / `rebar-observability.timer`. This creates an orphan deliverable: the Files section implies something new will be created, while the plan body says it already exists.
findings.location: Files section (last bullet) and Round-12 plan-review remediation > Probe deployment pinned

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise AC4 to explicitly state that S7 VERIFIES (not creates) the DLM retention policy owned by S1, e.g. 'The S1-provisioned DLM `retain_rule.count` (≥1, default 7) is confirmed in effect; S7 adds no duplicate DLM resource.'
findings.finding: AC4 states 'A DLM/`$site_path` snapshot retention policy is in effect (DLM `retain_rule.count` bounded; default 7)' without distinguishing ownership: Round-12 advisory clarifies that S1 OWNS the DLM policy and S7 only ASSERTS/monitors it (no second DLM resource). Without this ownership distinction in the AC, a reviewer cannot determine whether passing AC4 requires S7 to create the DLM resource or merely confirm S1's already exists — the criterion is ambiguous about what S7 must deliver vs. verify.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > AC4 and Round-12 plan-review remediation > Snapshot retention ownership

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
